quotes.text: Revenue Recognition The Company primarily earns revenue from sales of its energy storage systems and services including installation, commissioning and extended warranty services.

The OBBBA maintained tax credits available to manufacturers and include a credit for ten percent of qualified costs incurred to make electrode active materials in addition to credits of $35 per kWh of capacity for eligible battery cells and $10 per kWh of capacity for eligible battery modules.
The Company s growth strategy contemplates increasing sales of battery energy storage systems and related software and services through a direct sales team and sales channel partners.
Z3 battery modules have been shipped since the third quarter of 2023, and the transition reflects over fifteen years of accumulated insights and operational experience.
It serves as the system s intelligence layer and is designed to manage large numbers of battery modules in real time.

The system targets up to 1 GWh per acre, roughly four times most incumbent footprints, through stackable Indensity Core units that integrate Eos Z3 battery modules with the Eos DawnOS controls platform.
The Company expects its cost of goods sold to exceed revenues in the near term as it continues to scale production and prepares battery energy storage systems delivered to customers to go-live.

optionality.rationale: No excerpt mentions humanoid robotics or any new business segment related to the theme. The company focuses on energy storage systems and does not indicate investment in robotic battery packs.

revenueToOpportunity.rationale: Current revenue from energy storage systems is not compared to any theme opportunity. No mention of robotics TAM or how revenue relates to it.

catalystDensity.rationale: No near-term catalysts related to humanoid robotics are described. The excerpts discuss past shipments and technology but lack dated milestones for the theme.

managementConviction.rationale: Management language does not reference humanoid robotics or named programs for the theme. The filing describes general battery technology without commitment to robotics.

insiderConviction.rationale: Form 4: net $-18,638,187 over 4 buys and 27 sales by 3 insider(s), trailing 12 months.

customerValidation.rationale: Customer graph: gov awards $0, 0 named customer(s), 0 other filers name the company.

quotes.text: EXPLANATORY NOTE As previously announced, on December 31, 2025 (the Closing Date ), Matthews International Corporation, a Pennsylvania corporation ( Matthews or the Company ), completed the sale of its interests in Matthews Automation Solutions, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company and wholly-owned subsidiary of Matthews (the Transferred Entity ), and certain related assets to Duravant LLC (the Buyer ) pursuant to the terms of an Equity Purchase Agreement dated as of November 12, 2025 by and among Matthews and the Buyer, which was filed as Exhibit 2.1 to that Current Report on Form 8-K with the SEC on November 13, 2025 .

optionality.rationale: The company sold its automation solutions business, which is the segment relevant to warehouse automation and humanoid robotics. This divestiture indicates the company is not pursuing this theme; thus no new business creation.

revenueToOpportunity.rationale: With the sale of the automation solutions business, the company no longer has revenue exposure to warehouse automation. The theme opportunity is now irrelevant to the remaining operations.

catalystDensity.rationale: No catalysts related to humanoid robotics or warehouse automation are mentioned. The only event is the completion of a sale, which is a negative catalyst for the theme.

managementConviction.rationale: Management's action of selling the automation business demonstrates low conviction in the warehouse automation theme and no commitment to humanoid robotics.
